name: SuperTrend EMA Price Cross
title: SuperTrend + EMA Price Cross — Indicator by PiyushBhanKachwaha — TradingView
source: //@version=5indicator("SuperTrend + EMA Price Cross", overlay=true)// Inputsfactor    = input.float(3.0, "SuperTrend Factor", minval=0.1, step=0.1)atrLength = input.int(10, "ATR Length", minval=1)emaLength = input.int(200, "EMA Length", minval=1)// Calculations[supertrend, direction] = ta.supertrend(factor, atrLength)emaValue = ta.ema(close, emaLength)// Trend conditionsstBull = direction < 0stBear = direction > 0// Price crossing EMApriceCrossUp   = ta.crossover(close, emaValue)priceCrossDown = ta.crossunder(close, emaValue)// Final signalsbuySignal  = stBull and priceCrossUpsellSignal = stBear and priceCrossDown// Plotsplot(supertrend, "SuperTrend", color=stBull ? color.green : color.red, linewidth=2)plot(emaValue, "EMA", color=color.orange, linewidth=2)// Signal markersplotshape(buySignal, title="BUY", location=location.belowbar, style=shape.labelup, text="BUY", color=color.green, textcolor=color.white, size=size.small)plotshape(sellSignal, title="SELL", location=location.abovebar, style=shape.labeldown, text="SELL", color=color.red, textcolor=color.white, size=size.small)// Alertsalertcondition(buySignal, title="Buy Alert", message="BUY: Price crossed above EMA and SuperTrend is bullish on {{ticker}}")alertcondition(sellSignal, title="Sell Alert", message="SELL: Price crossed below EMA and SuperTrend is bearish on {{ticker}}")
description: SuperTrend + EMA Price Cross. Adjust the supertrend and EMA as per your requirements

name: AVVI Relative Strength Index
title: AVVI Relative Strength Index — Indicator by Omi3Trades — TradingView
source: //@version=6// It is a basic RSI Indicator with 40, 60 and 70 leels markedindicator(title="AVVI Relative Strength Index", shorttitle="AVVA RSI", format=format.price, precision=2, timeframe="", timeframe_gaps=true)rsiLengthInput = input.int(14, minval=1, title="RSI Length", group="RSI Settings")rsiSourceInput = input.source(close, "Source", group="RSI Settings")calculateDivergence = input.bool(false, title="Calculate Divergence", group="RSI Settings", display = display.none, tooltip = "Calculating divergences is needed in order for divergence alerts to fire.")change = ta.change(rsiSourceInput)up = ta.rma(math.max(change, 0), rsiLengthInput)down = ta.rma(-math.min(change, 0), rsiLengthInput)rsi = down == 0 ? 100 : up == 0 ? 0 : 100 - (100 / (1 + up / down))rsiPlot = plot(rsi, "RSI", color=color.rgb(255, 170, 170))rsiUpperBand = hline(80, "RSI Upper Band", color=#787B86)midline = hline(50, "RSI Middle Band", color=color.new(#787B86, 50))a40line = hline(40, "RSI 40Line", color=color.new(#ff0404, 60), linestyle = hline.style_solid)a60line = hline(60, "RSI 60Line", color=color.new(#2eeb09, 85), linestyle = hline.style_solid)a70line = hline(70, "RSI Middle Band", color=color.new(#0bf7f7, 70), linestyle = hline.style_solid)rsiLowerBand = hline(20, "RSI Lower Band", color=#787B86)fill(rsiUpperBand, rsiLowerBand, color=color.rgb(126, 87, 194, 90), title="RSI Background Fill")midLinePlot = plot(50, color = na, editable = false, display = display.none)a40LinePlot = plot(40, color = na, editable = false, display = display.none)a60LinePlot = plot(60, color = na, editable = false, display = display.none)a70LinePlot = plot(70, color = na, editable = false, display = display.none)fill(rsiPlot, midLinePlot, 100, 70, top_color = color.new(color.green, 0), bottom_color = color.new(color.green, 100),  title = "Overbought Gradient Fill")fill(rsiPlot, midLinePlot, 30,  0,  top_color = color.new(color.red, 100), bottom_color = color.new(color.red, 0),      title = "Oversold Gradient Fill")// Smoothing MA inputsGRP = "Smoothing"TT_BB = "Only applies when 'SMA + Bollinger Bands' is selected. Determines the distance between the SMA and the bands."maTypeInput = input.string("SMA", "Type", options = ["None", "SMA", "SMA + Bollinger Bands", "EMA", "SMMA (RMA)", "WMA", "VWMA"], group = GRP, display = display.none)var isBB = maTypeInput == "SMA + Bollinger Bands"maLengthInput = input.int(14, "Length", group = GRP, display = display.none, active = maTypeInput != "None")bbMultInput = input.float(2.0, "BB StdDev", minval = 0.001, maxval = 50, step = 0.5, tooltip = TT_BB, group = GRP, display = display.none, active = isBB)var enableMA = maTypeInput != "None"// Smoothing MA Calculationma(source, length, MAtype) =>    switch MAtype        "SMA"                   => ta.sma(source, length)        "SMA + Bollinger Bands" => ta.sma(source, length)        "EMA"                   => ta.ema(source, length)        "SMMA (RMA)"            => ta.rma(source, length)        "WMA"                   => ta.wma(source, length)        "VWMA"                  => ta.vwma(source, length)// Smoothing MA plotssmoothingMA = enableMA ? ma(rsi, maLengthInput, maTypeInput) : nasmoothingStDev = isBB ? ta.stdev(rsi, maLengthInput) * bbMultInput : naplot(smoothingMA, "RSI-based MA", color=color.rgb(59, 177, 255, 12), display = enableMA ? display.all : display.none, editable = enableMA)bbUpperBand = plot(smoothingMA + smoothingStDev, title = "Upper Bollinger Band", color=color.green, display = isBB ? display.all : display.none, editable = isBB)bbLowerBand = plot(smoothingMA - smoothingStDev, title = "Lower Bollinger Band", color=color.green, display = isBB ? display.all : display.none, editable = isBB)fill(bbUpperBand, bbLowerBand, color= isBB ? color.new(color.green, 90) : na, title="Bollinger Bands Background Fill", display = isBB ? display.all : display.none, editable = isBB)// DivergencelookbackRight = 5lookbackLeft = 5rangeUpper = 60rangeLower = 5bearColor = color.redbullColor = color.greentextColor = color.whitenoneColor = color.new(color.white, 100)_inRange(bool cond) =>    bars = ta.barssince(cond)    rangeLower <= bars and bars <= rangeUpperplFound = falsephFound = falsebullCond = falsebearCond = falsersiLBR = rsi[lookbackRight]if calculateDivergence    //------------------------------------------------------------------------------    // Regular Bullish    // rsi: Higher Low    plFound := not na(ta.pivotlow(rsi, lookbackLeft, lookbackRight))        rsiHL = rsiLBR > ta.valuewhen(plFound, rsiLBR, 1) and _inRange(plFound[1])    // Price: Lower Low    lowLBR = low[lookbackRight]    priceLL = lowLBR < ta.valuewhen(plFound, lowLBR, 1)    bullCond := priceLL and rsiHL and plFound    //------------------------------------------------------------------------------    // Regular Bearish    // rsi: Lower High    phFound := not na(ta.pivothigh(rsi, lookbackLeft, lookbackRight))    rsiLH = rsiLBR < ta.valuewhen(phFound, rsiLBR, 1) and _inRange(phFound[1])    // Price: Higher High    highLBR = high[lookbackRight]    priceHH = highLBR > ta.valuewhen(phFound, highLBR, 1)    bearCond := priceHH and rsiLH and phFoundplot(     plFound   ? rsiLBR : na,     offset    = -lookbackRight,     title     = "Regular Bullish",     linewidth = 2,     color     = (bullCond ? bullColor : noneColor),     display   = display.pane,     editable  = calculateDivergence)plotshape(     bullCond  ? rsiLBR : na,     offset    = -lookbackRight,     title     = "Regular Bullish Label",     text      = " Bull ",     style     = shape.labelup,     location  = location.absolute,     color     = bullColor,     textcolor = textColor,     display   = display.pane,     editable  = calculateDivergence)plot(     phFound   ? rsiLBR : na,     offset    = -lookbackRight,     title     = "Regular Bearish",     linewidth = 2,     color     = (bearCond ? bearColor : noneColor),     display   = display.pane,     editable  = calculateDivergence)plotshape(     bearCond  ? rsiLBR : na,     offset    = -lookbackRight,     title     = "Regular Bearish Label",     text      = " Bear ",     style     = shape.labeldown,     location  = location.absolute,     color     = bearColor,     textcolor = textColor,     display   = display.pane,     editable  = calculateDivergence)alertcondition(bullCond, title='Regular Bullish Divergence', message="Found a new Regular Bullish Divergence, `Pivot Lookback Right` number of bars to the left of the current bar.")alertcondition(bearCond, title='Regular Bearish Divergence', message='Found a new Regular Bearish Divergence, `Pivot Lookback Right` number of bars to the left of the current bar.')
description: Basic RSI, with 40, 60 and 70 levels marked and MA turned On.

title: Key Levels & Sessions — Indicator by Chefdantrades — TradingView
source: // This Pine Script® code is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public License 2.0 at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/// © LuxAlgo//@version=6indicator("Key Levels & Sessions", overlay = true, max_lines_count = 500, max_boxes_count = 500, max_labels_count = 500)// --- Constants & Inputs ---string TZ = "America/New_York"group_pd = "Previous Day Levels"color_pd = input.color(color.rgb(156, 39, 176), "PD High/Low Color", group = group_pd)color_poc = input.color(color.white, "PD POC Color", group = group_pd)group_asia = "Asia Session (20:00 - 02:00)"color_asia = input.color(#ff9800, "Asia Session Color", group = group_asia)group_london = "London Session (04:00 - 09:30)"color_london = input.color(#2196f3, "London Session Color", group = group_london)group_ny = "NY ORB (09:30 - 09:45)"color_ny = input.color(color.new(#5b9cf6, 80), "NY ORB Box Color", group = group_ny)color_ny_border = input.color(#5b9cf6, "NY ORB Border Color", group = group_ny)color_orb_mid = input.color(color.yellow, "ORB Midline Color", group = group_ny)// --- Time Calculations ---get_target_5pm() =>    y = year(time, TZ)    m = month(time, TZ)    d = dayofmonth(time, TZ)    h = hour(time, TZ)    base_5pm = timestamp(TZ, y, m, d, 17, 0, 0)    h >= 17 ? base_5pm + 86400000 : base_5pmtarget_5pm = get_target_5pm()// --- Object Tracking ---var line[]  all_lines  = array.new_line()var box[]   all_boxes  = array.new_box()var label[] all_labels = array.new_label()clear_drawings() =>    if array.size(all_lines) > 0        for l in all_lines            line.delete(l)        array.clear(all_lines)    if array.size(all_boxes) > 0        for b in all_boxes            box.delete(b)        array.clear(all_boxes)    if array.size(all_labels) > 0        for lb in all_labels            label.delete(lb)        array.clear(all_labels)// --- Logic ---is_cleanup_time = hour(time, TZ) == 17 and minute(time, TZ) == 0if is_cleanup_time and not is_cleanup_time[1]    clear_drawings()is_asia   = not na(time(timeframe.period, "2000-0200:1234567", TZ))is_london = not na(time(timeframe.period, "0400-0930:1234567", TZ))is_ny_orb = not na(time(timeframe.period, "0930-0945:1234567", TZ))is_active_day = not na(time(timeframe.period, "0000-1700:1234567", TZ))ny_day_change = ta.change(dayofmonth(time, TZ)) != 0is_new_day = ny_day_change// POC Calculationvar float[] daily_prices = array.new_float()var float[] daily_volumes = array.new_float()var float prev_poc = naif is_new_day    if array.size(daily_volumes) > 0        int max_idx = array.indexof(daily_volumes, array.max(daily_volumes))        prev_poc := array.get(daily_prices, max_idx)    array.clear(daily_prices)    array.clear(daily_volumes)if is_active_day    array.push(daily_prices, hl2)    array.push(daily_volumes, volume)// Session Trackingvar float asia_h = na, var float asia_l = navar float lon_h = na, var float lon_l = navar float orb_h = na, var float orb_l = naif is_asia    asia_h := not is_asia[1] ? high : math.max(high, asia_h)    asia_l := not is_asia[1] ? low : math.min(low, asia_l)if is_london    lon_h := not is_london[1] ? high : math.max(high, lon_h)    lon_l := not is_london[1] ? low : math.min(low, lon_l)if is_ny_orb    orb_h := not is_ny_orb[1] ? high : math.max(high, orb_h)    orb_l := not is_ny_orb[1] ? low : math.min(low, orb_l)// --- Drawing Variables ---var line l_pdh = na, var line l_pdl = na, var line l_poc = navar line l_asia_h = na, var line l_asia_l = navar line l_lon_h = na, var line l_lon_l = navar line l_orb_mid = navar box  b_orb = navar label lb_pdh = na, var label lb_pdl = na, var label lb_poc = navar label lb_asia_h = na, var label lb_asia_l = navar label lb_london_h = na, var label lb_london_l = navar label lb_orb_mid = na// --- Drawing Logic ---[s_pdh, s_pdl] = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "D", [high[1], low[1]], lookahead = barmerge.lookahead_on)// 1. PD Levels & POC (Labels on Right)if is_new_day    l_pdh := line.new(time, s_pdh, target_5pm, s_pdh, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_pd, width = 2)    l_pdl := line.new(time, s_pdl, target_5pm, s_pdl, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_pd, width = 2)    lb_pdh := label.new(target_5pm, s_pdh, "PDH", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_pd, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    lb_pdl := label.new(target_5pm, s_pdl, "PDL", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_pd, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    array.push(all_lines, l_pdh), array.push(all_lines, l_pdl)    array.push(all_labels, lb_pdh), array.push(all_labels, lb_pdl)    if not na(prev_poc)        l_poc := line.new(time, prev_poc, target_5pm, prev_poc, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_poc, width = 2)        lb_poc := label.new(target_5pm, prev_poc, "POC", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_poc, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)        array.push(all_lines, l_poc), array.push(all_labels, lb_poc)// 2. Asia (Labels on Right)if is_asia and not is_asia[1]    l_asia_h := line.new(time, asia_h, target_5pm, asia_h, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_asia, width = 2)    l_asia_l := line.new(time, asia_l, target_5pm, asia_l, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_asia, width = 2)    lb_asia_h := label.new(target_5pm, asia_h, "Asia High", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_asia, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    lb_asia_l := label.new(target_5pm, asia_l, "Asia Low", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_asia, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    array.push(all_lines, l_asia_h), array.push(all_lines, l_asia_l)    array.push(all_labels, lb_asia_h), array.push(all_labels, lb_asia_l)if not na(l_asia_h) and not is_cleanup_time    line.set_y1(l_asia_h, asia_h), line.set_y2(l_asia_h, asia_h)    line.set_y1(l_asia_l, asia_l), line.set_y2(l_asia_l, asia_l)    label.set_y(lb_asia_h, asia_h), label.set_y(lb_asia_l, asia_l)// 3. London (Labels on Right)if is_london and not is_london[1]    l_lon_h := line.new(time, lon_h, target_5pm, lon_h, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_london, width = 2)    l_lon_l := line.new(time, lon_l, target_5pm, lon_l, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_london, width = 2)    lb_london_h := label.new(target_5pm, lon_h, "London High", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_london, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    lb_london_l := label.new(target_5pm, lon_l, "London Low", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_london, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    array.push(all_lines, l_lon_h), array.push(all_lines, l_lon_l)    array.push(all_labels, lb_london_h), array.push(all_labels, lb_london_l)if not na(l_lon_h) and not is_cleanup_time    line.set_y1(l_lon_h, lon_h), line.set_y2(l_lon_h, lon_h)    line.set_y1(l_lon_l, lon_l), line.set_y2(l_lon_l, lon_l)    label.set_y(lb_london_h, lon_h), label.set_y(lb_london_l, lon_l)// 4. NY ORB (Labels on Right)if is_ny_orb and not is_ny_orb[1]    b_orb := box.new(time, orb_h, target_5pm, orb_l, xloc = xloc.bar_time, bgcolor = color_ny, border_color = color_ny_border)    l_orb_mid := line.new(time, (orb_h + orb_l) / 2, target_5pm, (orb_h + orb_l) / 2, xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = color_orb_mid, style = line.style_dashed)    lb_orb_mid := label.new(target_5pm, (orb_h + orb_l) / 2, "ORB Mid", xloc = xloc.bar_time, color = #00000000, textcolor = color_orb_mid, style = label.style_label_left, size = size.small)    array.push(all_boxes, b_orb), array.push(all_lines, l_orb_mid), array.push(all_labels, lb_orb_mid)if not na(b_orb) and not is_cleanup_time    box.set_top(b_orb, orb_h), box.set_bottom(b_orb, orb_l)    mid = (orb_h + orb_l) / 2    line.set_y1(l_orb_mid, mid), line.set_y2(l_orb_mid, mid)    label.set_y(lb_orb_mid, mid)
description: This is a professional-grade institutional key levels indicator designed for day traders who focus on session liquidity and high-volume price levels. The script provides a comprehensive view of the trading day by marking critical boundaries and institutional reference points, all strictly synchronized to the **America/New_York** (EST) timezone.### **Key Features:**#### **1. Institutional Daily Levels***   **Previous Day High (PDH) & Low (PDL):** Marked with solid purple lines, these represent the most basic yet powerful liquidity levels from the prior trading day.*   **Previous Day Point of Control (POC):** A solid white line calculated using a Volume Profile algorithm. It identifies the price level where the most trading activity occurred during the previous session, acting as a "magnet" for price.#### **2. Global Session Ranges***   **Asia Session (8:00 PM - 2:00 AM EST):** Plotted in orange. This captures the initial range of the global trading cycle.*   **London Session (4:00 AM - 9:30 AM EST):** Plotted in blue. This identifies the volatility often seen during the European open leading into the New York session.#### **3. New York 15-Minute Opening Range (ORB)***   **ORB Box:** Automatically captures the high and low of the first 15 minutes of the New York session (9:30 AM - 9:45 AM EST).*   **Midline Projection:** Features a yellow dashed midline within the ORB box, a critical level often used by traders to determine intraday bias.#### **4. Dynamic Projection & Cleanup***   **Extension to Market Close:** Once established, every level (Asia, London, ORB, and PD levels) automatically projects forward to the **5:00 PM EST market close**.*   **Automatic Reset:** To keep your workspace clean and professional, the indicator utilizes an object-tracking system that deletes all drawings from the previous day at exactly 5:00 PM EST, allowing the chart to "reset" for the new Asia session.*   **Right-Anchored Labels:** All levels are clearly labeled on the right side of th

title: XAUUSD Gold Sniper Dashboard PRO — Indicator by McMuscle87 — TradingView
source: //@version=5indicator("XAUUSD Gold Sniper Dashboard PRO", overlay=true)// =========================// SETTINGS// =========================step = input.float(50.0, "Psychological Level Step", step=0.1)swingLookback = input.int(50, "Discount Range Lookback", minval=10)// XAUUSD distance thresholdsnearEMA = input.float(4.0, "Near EMA Threshold")nearVWAP = input.float(5.0, "Near VWAP Threshold")stretchVWAP = input.float(15.0, "Stretched VWAP Threshold")overextendVWAP = input.float(25.0, "Overextended VWAP Threshold")// =========================// CORE CALCULATIONS// =========================ema50 = ta.ema(close, 50)vwapVal = ta.vwap(close)// DistancesdistEMA = close - ema50distVWAP = close - vwapValabsDistEMA = math.abs(distEMA)absDistVWAP = math.abs(distVWAP)// Nearest psych levelnearest50 = math.round(close / step) * stepdistTo50 = close - nearest50// =========================// DISCOUNT / PREMIUM ZONE// =========================rangeHigh = ta.highest(high, swingLookback)rangeLow = ta.lowest(low, swingLookback)equilibrium = (rangeHigh + rangeLow) / 2pdText = close < equilibrium ? "Discount Zone" : "Premium Zone"pdColor = close < equilibrium ? color.new(color.green, 0) : color.new(color.red, 0)// =========================// BIAS LOGIC// =========================bullBias = close > ema50 and close > vwapValbearBias = close < ema50 and close < vwapValbiasText = bullBias ? "Bullish Bias" :     bearBias ? "Bearish Bias" :     "Neutral / Chop"// =========================// VWAP CONDITION// =========================vwapState = absDistVWAP <= nearVWAP ? "Near VWAP" :     absDistVWAP <= stretchVWAP ? "Healthy Distance" :     absDistVWAP <= overextendVWAP ? "Stretched" :     "Overextended"vwapColor = absDistVWAP <= nearVWAP ? color.green :     absDistVWAP <= stretchVWAP ? color.lime :     absDistVWAP <= overextendVWAP ? color.orange :     color.red// =========================// EMA CONDITION// =========================emaState = absDistEMA <= nearEMA ? "Near EMA50" : "Extended from EMA50"emaColor = absDistEMA <= nearEMA ? color.green : color.orange// =========================// ENTRY QUALITY LOGIC// =========================entryText = "Wait"entryColor = color.orangehintText = "Wait for cleaner structure"// LONG conditionsgoodLongPullback = bullBias and close < equilibrium and absDistEMA <= nearEMA and absDistVWAP <= stretchVWAPokayLongPullback = bullBias and close < equilibrium and absDistEMA <= nearEMAchaseLong = bullBias and absDistVWAP > overextendVWAP// SHORT conditionsgoodShortPullback = bearBias and close > equilibrium and absDistEMA <= nearEMA and absDistVWAP <= stretchVWAPokayShortPullback = bearBias and close > equilibrium and absDistEMA <= nearEMAchaseShort = bearBias and absDistVWAP > overextendVWAPif goodLongPullback    entryText := "STRONG LONG ZONE"    entryColor := color.green    hintText := "Discount pullback near EMA/VWAP"else if okayLongPullback    entryText := "OKAY LONG ZONE"    entryColor := color.lime    hintText := "Good pullback, wait for confirmation"else if chaseLong    entryText := "CHASE WARNING"    entryColor := color.red    hintText := "Bullish but too stretched from VWAP"else if goodShortPullback    entryText := "STRONG SHORT ZONE"    entryColor := color.red    hintText := "Premium pullback near EMA/VWAP"else if okayShortPullback    entryText := "OKAY SHORT ZONE"    entryColor := color.orange    hintText := "Good short area, wait for confirmation"else if chaseShort    entryText := "CHASE WARNING"    entryColor := color.red    hintText := "Bearish but too stretched from VWAP"else    entryText := "WAIT"    entryColor := color.gray    hintText := "No clean setup right now"// =========================// PLOTS// =========================plot(ema50, title="EMA 50", color=color.yellow, linewidth=2)plot(vwapVal, title="VWAP", color=color.aqua, linewidth=2)plot(equilibrium, title="Equilibrium (50%)", color=color.fuchsia, linewidth=2, style=plot.style_line)// =========================// DASHBOARD TABLE// =========================var table dash = table.new(position.top_right, 1, 11, border_width=1)if barstate.islast    table.cell(dash, 0, 0, "Price: " + str.tostring(close, format.mintick), text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.black)    table.cell(dash, 0, 1, "EMA 50: " + str.tostring(ema50, format.mintick), text_color=color.black, bgcolor=color.yellow)    table.cell(dash, 0, 2, "VWAP: " + str.tostring(vwapVal, format.mintick), text_color=color.black, bgcolor=color.aqua)    table.cell(dash, 0, 3, "Dist EMA: " + str.tostring(distEMA, format.mintick), text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.gray)    table.cell(dash, 0, 4, "Dist VWAP: " + str.tostring(distVWAP, format.mintick), text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.gray)    table.cell(dash, 0, 5, "EMA State: " + emaState, text_color=color.white, bgcolor=emaColor)    table.cell(dash, 0, 6, "VWAP State: " + vwapState, text_color=color.white, bgcolor=vwapColor)    table.cell(dash, 0, 7, "Nearest $50: " + str.tostring(nearest50, format.mintick), text_color=color.black, bgcolor=color.orange)    table.cell(dash, 0, 8, "Dist to $50: " + str.tostring(distTo50, format.mintick), text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.gray)    table.cell(dash, 0, 9, pdText + " | EQ: " + str.tostring(equilibrium, format.mintick), text_color=color.white, bgcolor=pdColor)    table.cell(dash, 0, 10, biasText + " | " + entryText + " | " + hintText,         text_color=color.white,         bgcolor=entryColor)
description: A precision-built trading dashboard designed specifically for XAUUSD (Gold), combining institutional concepts like EMA, VWAP, Premium/Discount zones, and psychological levels into one clear, actionable view.This tool helps traders quickly identify market bias, entry quality, and price positioning—eliminating guesswork and improving decision-making in fast-moving gold markets.🔑 Key Features• Market Bias DetectionAutomatically defines Bullish, Bearish, or Neutral conditionsBased on price relative to EMA 50 and VWAP• Smart Entry ZonesHighlights:✅ Strong Long / Short Zones⚠️ Okay Pullbacks🚫 Chase Warnings (overextended moves)Built around pullbacks to value (EMA + VWAP confluence)• VWAP & EMA Distance LogicTracks how far price is from key levelsLabels conditions as:NearHealthyStretchedOverextended• Premium / Discount FrameworkUses a dynamic range to identify:🟢 Discount (buy-side interest)🔴 Premium (sell-side interest)Displays equilibrium (50% level)• Psychological Levels ($50 increments)Automatically calculates nearest key levelHelps identify reaction zones and liquidity areas• Real-Time DashboardClean table showing:Price, EMA, VWAPDistances to key levelsMarket biasEntry quality + trading hint📊 How to UseLook for alignment between bias + zone + VWAP/EMA proximityFocus on:Discount + Bullish Bias → Long setupsPremium + Bearish Bias → Short setupsAvoid trades when:Market is overextendedDashboard shows “Wait” or “Chase Warning”⚙️ Best ForIntraday & scalping tradersXAUUSD specialistsTraders using smart money concepts (SMC), VWAP, and mean reversion

name: ATR Multiple From MA
title: ATR % Multiple From MA — Indicator by xTradeLogicx — TradingView
source: //@version=6indicator("ATR % Multiple From MA", shorttitle="ATR Dist MA", overlay=true)maType = input.string("EMA", "MA Type", options=["EMA", "SMA"])maLength = input.int(50, "MA Length", minval=1)atrLength = input.int(14, "ATR Length", minval=1)showTable = input.bool(true, "Show Stats Table")showMA = input.bool(true, "Plot Reference MA")maColor = input.color(color.orange, "MA Color")textColor = input.color(color.rgb(255, 190, 92), "Table Text Color")bgColor = input.color(color.new(color.black, 100), "Table Background")frameColor = input.color(color.new(color.gray, 85), "Table Frame Color")ma = maType == "EMA" ? ta.ema(close, maLength) : ta.sma(close, maLength)atr = ta.atr(atrLength)atrPct = close != 0 ? (atr / close) * 100 : napctGainFromMA = ma != 0 ? ((close - ma) / ma) * 100 : naatrPctMultipleFromMA = atrPct != 0 ? pctGainFromMA / atrPct : naatrMultipleFromMA = atr != 0 ? (close - ma) / atr : naplot(showMA ? ma : na, "Reference MA", color=maColor, linewidth=2)var table stats = table.new(position.middle_right, 2, 3, bgcolor=bgColor, frame_color=frameColor, frame_width=1)if barstate.islast    if showTable        table.cell(stats, 0, 0, "ATR% Multiple From MA", text_color=textColor, text_size=size.small, bgcolor=bgColor)        table.cell(stats, 1, 0, na(atrPctMultipleFromMA) ? "n/a" : str.tostring(atrPctMultipleFromMA, "#.##") + "x", text_color=textColor, text_size=size.small, bgcolor=bgColor)        table.cell(stats, 0, 1, "% Gain From MA", text_color=textColor, text_size=size.small, bgcolor=bgColor)        table.cell(stats, 1, 1, na(pctGainFromMA) ? "n/a" : str.tostring(pctGainFromMA, "#.##") + "%", text_color=textColor, text_size=size.small, bgcolor=bgColor)        table.cell(stats, 0, 2, "ATR %", text_color=textColor, text_size=size.small, bgcolor=bgColor)        table.cell(stats, 1, 2, na(atrPct) ? "n/a" : str.tostring(atrPct, "#.##") + "%", text_color=textColor, text_size=size.small, bgcolor=bgColor)    else        table.clear(stats, 0, 0, 1, 2)alertcondition(ta.crossover(atrMultipleFromMA, 2), "ATR Distance Above 2x", "Price moved above 2 ATRs from the selected MA")alertcondition(ta.crossunder(atrMultipleFromMA, -2), "ATR Distance Below -2x", "Price moved below -2 ATRs from the selected MA")
description: ATR % Multiple From MA OF YOUR CHOICE. Gives you the ATR% multiple from your chosen EMA

name: Futrues Daily Pivot Zones
title: Futrues Daily Pivot Zones — Indicator by scottjc77 — TradingView
source: //@version=5indicator("Futures Daily Pivot Zones", overlay=true)// ─── INPUTS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────zoneBuffer  = input.float(6.0, "Zone Buffer (points)", minval=0.5, step=0.5)zoneExtend  = input.int(500,   "Extend Right (bars)",  minval=10,  maxval=1000)showPP      = input.bool(true, "Show Pivot Point (PP)")showR1      = input.bool(true, "Show R1")showR2      = input.bool(true, "Show R2")showR3      = input.bool(true, "Show R3")showS1      = input.bool(true, "Show S1")showS2      = input.bool(true, "Show S2")showS3      = input.bool(true, "Show S3")showPDHL    = input.bool(true, "Show Prev Day High/Low")ppColor     = input.color(color.new(color.gray,  75), "PP Color")resColor    = input.color(color.new(color.red,    75), "Resistance Color")supColor    = input.color(color.new(color.green,  75), "Support Color")pdhlColor   = input.color(color.new(color.aqua,   75), "Prev Day High/Low Color")// ─── GET PREVIOUS DAY DATA ────────────────────────────────────────────────────prevHigh  = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "D", high[1],  lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_on)prevLow   = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "D", low[1],   lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_on)prevClose = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "D", close[1], lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_on)// ─── CALCULATE PIVOT POINTS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────PP = (prevHigh + prevLow + prevClose) / 3R1 = 2 * PP - prevLowR2 = PP + (prevHigh - prevLow)R3 = prevHigh + 2 * (PP - prevLow)S1 = 2 * PP - prevHighS2 = PP - (prevHigh - prevLow)S3 = prevLow - 2 * (prevHigh - PP)// ─── DRAW FUNCTION ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────drawZone(lvl, col, lbl) =>    if barstate.islast        leftBar  = bar_index - 750        rightBar = bar_index + zoneExtend        box.new(             left         = leftBar,             top          = lvl + zoneBuffer,             right        = rightBar,             bottom       = lvl - zoneBuffer,             border_color = col,             border_width  = 1,             bgcolor       = col,             text          = lbl,             text_color    = color.white,             text_size     = size.small)// ─── DRAW MAIN PIVOTS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────if showPP    drawZone(PP, ppColor, "PP")if showR1    drawZone(R1, resColor, "R1")if showR2    drawZone(R2, resColor, "R2")if showR3    drawZone(R3, resColor, "R3")if showS1    drawZone(S1, supColor, "S1")if showS2    drawZone(S2, supColor, "S2")if showS3    drawZone(S3, supColor, "S3")// ─── DRAW PREV DAY HIGH/LOW ───────────────────────────────────────────────────if showPDHL    drawZone(prevHigh, pdhlColor, "PDH")    drawZone(prevLow,  pdhlColor, "PDL")
description: Automatically plots Daily Pivot Point levels as clean rectangle zones directly on your futures chart. Levels are calculated from the previous day's High, Low, and Close — the same levels watched by institutions, banks, and algorithms every single session.Levels Included:PP — Main Pivot Point (most watched level of the day)R1, R2, R3 — Resistance zones above PPS1, S2, S3 — Support zones below PPPDH — Previous Day HighPDL — Previous Day LowHow To Use:Look for price to reject or bounce off a zone in confluence with your EMA bias and VWAP direction. The more factors that agree at a zone — the stronger the trade.Recommended Zone Buffer By Instrument:MES (Micro E-Mini S&P 500) → 4.0 - 5.0 pointsMNQ (Micro E-Mini Nasdaq) → 8.0 - 10.0 pointsMGC (Micro Gold) → 15.0 - 20.0 pointsMCL (Micro Crude Oil) → 0.15 - 0.25 pointsM2K (Micro Russell 2000) → 3.0 - 4.0 pointsRecommended Lookback:750 bars on a 15-min chart covers approximately 3 trading days of price action.Release NotesAutomatically plots Daily Pivot Point levels as clean rectangle zones directly on your futures chart. name: ORB ORB TRADING STRATEGY
title: ORB+ - ORB TRADING STRATEGY — Indicator by ulysseslacson — TradingView
source: //@version=6indicator("ORB+", overlay=true)// =========================// Inputs// =========================marketType     = input.string("STOCKS", "Market Type", options=["FOREX", "GOLD", "STOCKS"])orbTz          = input.string("America/New_York", "Timezone")useManualStart = input.bool(false, "Manual Start Override")manualHour     = input.int(9, "Manual Start Hour", minval=0, maxval=23)manualMinute   = input.int(30, "Manual Start Minute", minval=0, maxval=59)orbBars        = input.int(3, "Number of M5 Bars", minval=1, maxval=12)showBox        = input.bool(true, "Show ORB Box")showMid        = input.bool(false, "Show Midline")highColor      = input.color(color.lime, "ORB High Color")lowColor       = input.color(color.red, "ORB Low Color")midColor       = input.color(color.yellow, "Midline Color")boxColor       = input.color(color.new(color.blue, 88), "Box Fill Color")lineWidth      = input.int(2, "Line Width", minval=1, maxval=5)// =========================// Auto Start Time by Market Type// FOREX = 8:00 ET// GOLD  = 8:00 ET// STOCKS = 9:30 ET// =========================int autoHour   = marketType == "STOCKS" ? 9 : 8int autoMinute = marketType == "STOCKS" ? 30 : 0int startHour   = useManualStart ? manualHour : autoHourint startMinute = useManualStart ? manualMinute : autoMinute// Best use: M5 or lowerif not timeframe.isintraday    runtime.error("ORB+ works on intraday charts only.")if timeframe.in_seconds() > timeframe.in_seconds("5")    runtime.error("Use ORB+ on 5-minute or lower timeframes.")// =========================// ORB logic in 5-minute context// =========================f_orbHigh(_startHour, _startMinute, _orbBars, _tz) =>    var float orbHigh = na    var int count = 0    d  = dayofmonth(time, _tz)    mo = month(time, _tz)    y  = year(time, _tz)    h  = hour(time, _tz)    m  = minute(time, _tz)    newDay = (ta.change(d) != 0) or (ta.change(mo) != 0) or (ta.change(y) != 0)    if newDay        orbHigh := na        count := 0    totalStart = _startHour * 60 + _startMinute    totalNow   = h * 60 + m    inWindow   = totalNow >= totalStart and totalNow < totalStart + (_orbBars * 5)    if inWindow and count < _orbBars        count += 1        orbHigh := na(orbHigh) ? high : math.max(orbHigh, high)    count >= _orbBars ? orbHigh : naf_orbLow(_startHour, _startMinute, _orbBars, _tz) =>    var float orbLow = na    var int count = 0    d  = dayofmonth(time, _tz)    mo = month(time, _tz)    y  = year(time, _tz)    h  = hour(time, _tz)    m  = minute(time, _tz)    newDay = (ta.change(d) != 0) or (ta.change(mo) != 0) or (ta.change(y) != 0)    if newDay        orbLow := na        count := 0    totalStart = _startHour * 60 + _startMinute    totalNow   = h * 60 + m    inWindow   = totalNow >= totalStart and totalNow < totalStart + (_orbBars * 5)    if inWindow and count < _orbBars        count += 1        orbLow := na(orbLow) ? low : math.min(orbLow, low)    count >= _orbBars ? orbLow : naf_orbReady(_startHour, _startMinute, _orbBars, _tz) =>    var int count = 0    d  = dayofmonth(time, _tz)    mo = month(time, _tz)    y  = year(time, _tz)    h  = hour(time, _tz)    m  = minute(time, _tz)    newDay = (ta.change(d) != 0) or (ta.change(mo) != 0) or (ta.change(y) != 0)    if newDay        count := 0    totalStart = _startHour * 60 + _startMinute    totalNow   = h * 60 + m    inWindow   = totalNow >= totalStart and totalNow < totalStart + (_orbBars * 5)    if inWindow and count < _orbBars        count += 1    count >= _orbBars// =========================// Pull M5 ORB values so it stays identical on M1/M3/M5// =========================orbHigh = request.security(     syminfo.tickerid,     "5",     f_orbHigh(startHour, startMinute, orbBars, orbTz),     barmerge.gaps_off,     barmerge.lookahead_off)orbLow = request.security(     syminfo.tickerid,     "5",     f_orbLow(startHour, startMinute, orbBars, orbTz),     barmerge.gaps_off,     barmerge.lookahead_off)orbReady = request.security(     syminfo.tickerid,     "5",     f_orbReady(startHour, startMinute, orbBars, orbTz),     barmerge.gaps_off,     barmerge.lookahead_off)orbMid = orbReady ? (orbHigh + orbLow) / 2.0 : na// =========================// Plot levels// =========================pHigh = plot(orbReady ? orbHigh : na, "ORB High", color=highColor, linewidth=lineWidth, style=plot.style_linebr)pLow  = plot(orbReady ? orbLow  : na, "ORB Low",  color=lowColor,  linewidth=lineWidth, style=plot.style_linebr)pMid  = plot(showMid and orbReady ? orbMid : na, "ORB Mid", color=midColor, linewidth=1, style=plot.style_linebr)fill(pHigh, pLow, color=showBox and orbReady ? boxColor : na)// =========================// Alerts// =========================bullBreak = orbReady and ta.crossover(close, orbHigh)bearBreak = orbReady and ta.crossunder(close, orbLow)alertcondition(bullBreak, "ORB Break Above", "Price broke above ORB High")alertcondition(bearBreak, "ORB Break Below", "Price broke below ORB Low")
description: ORB+ is an Opening Range Breakout indicator built to automatically mark the session opening range for FOREX, GOLD, and STOCKS using completed 5-minute candles only. It is designed for traders who want a clean and consistent way to define the early-session range and use it as a breakout framework.The indicator lets you choose between FOREX, GOLD, and STOCKS. Based on your selection, ORB+ automatically sets the default opening range start time in New York time. For FOREX and GOLD, the default start is 8:00 AM ET. For STOCKS, the default start is 9:30 AM ET. This allows the indicator to adapt to the market you are trading without needing to manually change the session logic each time.ORB+ builds the range using M5 data, even if you are viewing the chart on M1 or another lower timeframe. This keeps the opening range fixed and prevents it from changing between timeframes. By default, the indicator uses 3 five-minute candles, which creates a 15-minute opening range. The ORB levels only appear after the full range is complete.For example, in STOCKS mode, the opening range starts at 9:30 AM ET and uses the first three 5-minute candles. The ORB High and ORB Low will only appear after that 15-minute window is finished. On lower timeframes like M1, the indicator will still wait for the full opening range to complete before plotting the levels.How To Use:Apply ORB+ to an intraday chart and keep the chart timeframe at 5 minutes or lower. Select your market type: FOREX, GOLD, or STOCKS. The indicator will then automatically use the correct default start time for that market. If you want a custom opening range start, enable Manual Start Override and enter your preferred hour and minute.You can also adjust the Number of M5 Bars input. A value of 3 creates a 15-minute opening range, while a larger value creates a wider range. Once complete, the indicator plots the ORB High and ORB Low on the chart. source: //@version=6//By Henrioque Centieiroindicator("Multi-Sector Oil Correlation Tracker", shorttitle="Oil-Sector", overlay=false)// ============================================================================// INPUT PARAMETERS// ============================================================================// Oil ticker symboloil_symbol = input.string("NYMEX:CL1!", "Oil Symbol", group="Data Sources")// Sector symbolsenergy_symbol = input.string("AMEX:XLE", "Energy Sector", group="Sectors")transport_symbol = input.string("AMEX:XTN", "Transportation Sector", group="Sectors")consumer_symbol = input.string("AMEX:XLY", "Consumer Discretionary", group="Sectors")finance_symbol = input.string("AMEX:XLF", "Financial Sector", group="Sectors")tech_symbol = input.string("NASDAQ:QQQ", "Technology Sector", group="Sectors")industrial_symbol = input.string("AMEX:XLI", "Industrial Sector", group="Sectors")// Correlation settingscorrelation_period = input.int(60, "Correlation Period (bars)", minval=2, maxval=1000, group="Correlation Settings")// Display optionsshow_labels = input.bool(true, "Show Sector Labels", group="Display")show_table = input.bool(true, "Show Correlation Table", group="Display")table_position = input.string("Top Right", "Table Position",     options=["Top Left", "Top Center", "Top Right", "Middle Left", "Middle Center", "Middle Right",              "Bottom Left", "Bottom Center", "Bottom Right"], group="Display")table_size = input.string("Normal", "Table Size", options=["Tiny", "Small", "Normal", "Large", "Huge"], group="Display")label_size = input.string("Small", "Label Size", options=["Tiny", "Small", "Normal", "Large"], group="Display")line_width = input.int(2, "Line Width", minval=1, maxval=5, group="Display")show_fill = input.bool(true, "Show Energy Fill", group="Display")// ============================================================================// FUNCTIONS// ============================================================================// Function to calculate Pearson correlation coefficientpearson_correlation(x, y, length) =>    mean_x = ta.sma(x, length)    mean_y = ta.sma(y, length)    numerator = ta.sma((x - mean_x) * (y - mean_y), length)    denominator = math.sqrt(ta.sma(math.pow(x - mean_x, 2), length) * ta.sma(math.pow(y - mean_y, 2), length))    correlation = denominator != 0 ? numerator / denominator : 0    correlation// Function to get correlation strength labelget_strength_label(corr) =>    abs_corr = math.abs(corr)    if abs_corr > 0.7        "Very Strong"    else if abs_corr > 0.5        "Strong"    else if abs_corr > 0.3        "Moderate"    else if abs_corr > 0.1        "Weak"    else        "None"// Function to get correlation colorget_corr_color(corr) =>    if corr > 0.5        color.new(color.green, 30)    else if corr > 0.3        color.new(color.green, 60)    else if corr > -0.3        color.new(color.gray, 60)    else if corr > -0.5        color.new(color.red, 60)    else        color.new(color.red, 30)// Function to get table positionget_table_position(pos) =>    switch pos        "Top Left" => position.top_left        "Top Center" => position.top_center        "Top Right" => position.top_right        "Middle Left" => position.middle_left        "Middle Center" => position.middle_center        "Middle Right" => position.middle_right        "Bottom Left" => position.bottom_left        "Bottom Center" => position.bottom_center        "Bottom Right" => position.bottom_right        => position.top_right// Function to get table sizeget_table_size(sz) =>    switch sz        "Tiny" => size.tiny        "Small" => size.small        "Normal" => size.normal        "Large" => size.large        "Huge" => size.huge        => size.normal// Function to get label sizeget_label_size(sz) =>    switch sz        "Tiny" => size.tiny        "Small" => size.small        "Normal" => size.normal        "Large" => size.large        => size.small// ============================================================================// FETCH DATA// ============================================================================// Get oil priceoil_close = request.security(oil_symbol, timeframe.period, close)// Get sector pricesenergy_close = request.security(energy_symbol, timeframe.period, close)transport_close = request.security(transport_symbol, timeframe.period, close)consumer_close = request.security(consumer_symbol, timeframe.period, close)finance_close = request.security(finance_symbol, timeframe.period, close)tech_close = request.security(tech_symbol, timeframe.period, close)industrial_close = request.security(industrial_symbol, timeframe.period, close)// ============================================================================// CALCULATE RETURNS// ============================================================================// Calculate percentage returnsoil_return = ta.roc(oil_close, 1)energy_return = ta.roc(energy_close, 1)transport_return = ta.roc(transport_close, 1)consumer_return = ta.roc(consumer_close, 1)finance_return = ta.roc(finance_close, 1)tech_return = ta.roc(tech_close, 1)industrial_return = ta.roc(industrial_close, 1)// ============================================================================// CORRELATION CALCULATIONS// ============================================================================// Calculate correlations with oilcorr_energy = pearson_correlation(oil_return, energy_return, correlation_period)corr_transport = pearson_correlation(oil_return, transport_return, correlation_period)corr_consumer = pearson_correlation(oil_return, consumer_return, correlation_period)corr_finance = pearson_correlation(oil_return, finance_return, correlation_period)corr_tech = pearson_correlation(oil_return, tech_return, correlation_period)corr_industrial = pearson_correlation(oil_return, industrial_return, correlation_period)// ============================================================================// PLOTTING// ============================================================================// Plot correlation lines with distinct colors and user-defined widthp1 = plot(corr_energy, "Energy", color=color.new(color.green, 0), linewidth=line_width)p2 = plot(corr_transport, "Transportation", color=color.new(color.red, 0), linewidth=line_width)p3 = plot(corr_consumer, "Consumer", color=color.new(color.blue, 0), linewidth=line_width)p4 = plot(corr_finance, "Finance", color=color.new(color.orange, 0), linewidth=line_width)p5 = plot(corr_tech, "Technology", color=color.new(color.purple, 0), linewidth=line_width)p6 = plot(corr_industrial, "Industrial", color=color.new(color.teal, 0), linewidth=line_width)// Zero line as plot (for fill)p_zero = plot(0, "Zero Line", color=color.gray, linewidth=2)// Reference lineshline(0.5, "Strong Positive", color=color.new(color.green, 70), linestyle=hline.style_dashed)hline(-0.5, "Strong Negative", color=color.new(color.red, 70), linestyle=hline.style_dashed)hline(0.3, "Moderate Positive", color=color.new(color.green, 85), linestyle=hline.style_dotted)hline(-0.3, "Moderate Negative", color=color.new(color.red, 85), linestyle=hline.style_dotted)// Fill between zero line and energy correlation (optional)fill(p1, p_zero, color=show_fill ? (corr_energy > 0 ? color.new(color.green, 90) : color.new(color.red, 90)) : na, title="Energy Fill")// Add labels for current values with user-defined sizeif show_labels and barstate.islast    lbl_size = get_label_size(label_size)    label.new(bar_index + 5, corr_energy, "Energy: " + str.tostring(corr_energy, "#.##"),              color=color.new(color.green, 20), textcolor=color.white, style=label.style_label_left, size=lbl_size)    label.new(bar_index + 5, corr_transport, "Transport: " + str.tostring(corr_transport, "#.##"),              color=color.new(color.red, 20), textcolor=color.white, style=label.style_label_left, size=lbl_size)    label.new(bar_index + 5, corr_consumer, "Consumer: " + str.tostring(corr_consumer, "#.##"),              color=color.new(color.blue, 20), textcolor=color.white, style=label.style_label_left, size=lbl_size)    label.new(bar_index + 5, corr_finance, "Finance: " + str.tostring(corr_finance, "#.##"),              color=color.new(color.orange, 20), textcolor=color.white, style=label.style_label_left, size=lbl_size)    label.new(bar_index + 5, corr_tech, "Tech: " + str.tostring(corr_tech, "#.##"),              color=color.new(color.purple, 20), textcolor=color.white, style=label.style_label_left, size=lbl_size)    label.new(bar_index + 5, corr_industrial, "Industrial: " + str.tostring(corr_industrial, "#.##"),              color=color.new(color.teal, 20), textcolor=color.white, style=label.style_label_left, size=lbl_size)// ============================================================================// CORRELATION TABLE// ============================================================================if show_table and barstate.islast    tbl_pos = get_table_position(table_position)    tbl_size = get_table_size(table_size)    var table corr_table = table.new(tbl_pos, 3, 7, bgcolor=color.new(color.black, 80),         border_width=1, border_color=color.gray)    // Header    table.cell(corr_table, 0, 0, "Sector", text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.new(color.blue, 50), text_size=tbl_size)    table.cell(corr_table, 1, 0, "Correlation", text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.new(color.blue, 50), text_size=tbl_size)    table.cell(corr_table, 2, 0, "Strength", text_color=color.white, bgcolor=color.new(color.blue, 50), text_size=tbl_size)    // Energy    table.cell(corr_table, 0, 1, "Energy", text_color=color.white, text_size=tbl_size)    table.cell(corr_table, 1, 1, str.tostring(corr_energy, "#.##"),               text_color=color.white, bgcolor=get_corr_color(corr_energy), text_size=tbl_size)    table.cell(corr_table, 2, 1, get_strength_label(corr_energy), text_color=color.white, text_size=tbl_size)    // Transportation    table.cell(corr_table, 0, 2, "Transport", text_color=color.white, text_size=tbl_size)    table.cell(corr_tabl
description: I've been always interested in the correlation between oil prices and the different sectors, but after writing the article "8 Reasons Why the 2026 Oil Shock Is Different From the 1970s Crisis", I felt that I needed to go a bit further in understanding these correlations.  This Multi-Sector Oil Correlation Tracker tracks real-time correlations between oil prices and major market sectors, helping you to identify sector rotation opportunities and oil price impacts.HOW IT WORKSCalculates Pearson correlation coefficients between crude oil CL1! (you can replace with USOIL ) and six key sectors:Energy (XLE) - Typically positively correlatedTransportation (XTN) - Often negatively correlated (fuel costs)Consumer Discretionary (XLY) - Sentiment indicatorFinancials (XLF) - Economic health gaugeTechnology (QQQ) - Risk appetite proxyIndustrials (XLI) - Economic activity measureCorrelations range from -1 (perfect negative) to +1 (perfect positive). Values above ±0.5 indicate strong relationships.HOW TO USESTRONG POSITIVE (>0.5): Sector moves with oil - consider energy/inflation playsSTRONG NEGATIVE (<-0.5): Sector moves against oil - defensive positioningWEAK CORRELATION (-0.3 to 0.3): Decoupling - sector-specific factors dominateWatch for correlation shifts at the reference lines (±0.3, ±0.5) to spot regime changes.USE CASESSector Rotation Strategy: Identify which sectors benefit/suffer from oil movesPortfolio Hedging: Find negatively correlated sectors for balanceRisk Management: Monitor when correlations spike (systemic risk)Energy Trade Timing: Confirm energy sector entries when correlation is strongMacro Analysis: Understand oil's current influence on different parts of the marketBest used on daily or 4-hour timeframes for reliable statistical relationships.The chart above illustrates well how USOIL is correlated with XLE, and the indicator shows indeed a 0.46 correlation. Let me know if you have suggestions and comments.- Henrique Centieiro

source: // ============================================================//  SWING STRUCTURE SYSTEM v7//  - CHoCH anchored stdv grid per session//  - Current session: updates to most recent CHoCH//  - Past sessions: grid stays frozen (not deleted)// ============================================================//@version=6indicator("Swing Structure System v7", overlay=true, max_lines_count=500, max_labels_count=500, max_boxes_count=200)// ─────────────────────────────────────────────// INPUTS// ─────────────────────────────────────────────swingLen    = input.int(10,    "Swing Length",      minval=3, maxval=50,          group="🔧 Structure")showLabels  = input.bool(true, "Show 0 / 1 Labels",                               group="🔧 Structure")showLevels  = input.bool(true, "Show Stdv Levels",                                 group="🔧 Structure")showCHoCH   = input.bool(true, "Show CHoCH Line",                                  group="🔧 Structure")showOB      = input.bool(true, "Show Order Blocks",                                group="🔧 Structure")extBars     = input.int(60,    "Extension (bars right)", minval=20, maxval=200,    group="🔧 Structure")showAsian   = input.bool(true, "Asian Session",  group="🕐 Sessions")showLondon  = input.bool(true, "London Session", group="🕐 Sessions")showNYAM    = input.bool(true, "New York AM",    group="🕐 Sessions")showNYPM    = input.bool(true, "New York PM",    group="🕐 Sessions")asianColor  = input.color(color.new(#c62828, 80), "Asian Color",   group="🎨 Colors")londonColor = input.color(color.new(#00838f, 80), "London Color",  group="🎨 Colors")nyAMColor   = input.color(color.new(#e65100, 80), "NY AM Color",   group="🎨 Colors")nyPMColor   = input.color(color.new(#283593, 80), "NY PM Color",   group="🎨 Colors")bullColor   = input.color(#00e5ff,                "Bullish Color", group="🎨 Colors")bearColor   = input.color(#ff1744,                "Bearish Color", group="🎨 Colors")obBullColor = input.color(color.new(#00e5ff, 75), "Bull OB Color", group="🎨
